justification: This experimental paper replicating herd behavior studies with LLMs requires rigorous evaluation of experimental design and causal inference (Econometrician), careful assessment of AI agent implementation and prompt engineering (Data_Scientist), and critical analysis of financial stability implications for regulatory policy (Policymaker).

This experimental paper requires an Econometrician to evaluate the causal inference design and behavioral experiment methodology, an ML_Expert to assess the LLM implementation choices and prompt engineering validity, and a Policymaker to evaluate the financial stability implications and real-world applicability of AI-powered trading insights.

This experimental paper testing AI trading behavior requires rigorous evaluation of causal inference and experimental design (Econometrician), careful assessment of LLM implementation and prompt engineering methodology (Data_Scientist), and critical analysis of financial stability implications for regulatory policy (Policymaker).

This experimental paper using LLMs to simulate trading behavior requires rigorous evaluation of causal inference methodology and experimental design (Econometrician), careful assessment of data generation/manipulation from AI agents and prompt engineering (Data_Scientist), and critical analysis of financial stability implications for regulatory policy (Policymaker).

This experimental paper testing LLM trading behavior requires an Econometrician to evaluate the experimental design and causal claims about AI vs. human decision-making, a Policymaker to assess financial stability implications and regulatory relevance, and an ML_Expert to scrutinize the LLM implementation choices and prompt engineering methodology.

This experimental paper replicating behavioral finance studies with LLMs requires rigorous evaluation of the experimental design and causal inference framework (Econometrician), assessment of financial stability implications and regulatory relevance (Policymaker), and technical validation of LLM implementation choices including temperature settings, prompt engineering, and model selection (ML_Expert).

This experimental paper replicating classic behavioral finance studies with LLMs requires rigorous evaluation of experimental design and causal inference (Econometrician), careful assessment of data manipulation and LLM implementation choices (Data_Scientist), and critical analysis of financial stability implications for real-world policy (Policymaker).

This experimental study comparing AI and human trading behavior requires rigorous evaluation of the experimental design and causal interpretation (Econometrician), assessment of financial stability implications for real-world policy (Policymaker), and consideration of AI bias, fairness in training data, and implications of algorithmic decision-making in markets (Ethicist).

This experimental paper replicating behavioral finance studies with LLMs requires rigorous evaluation of the experimental design and causal interpretation (Econometrician), assessment of financial stability implications for regulation (Policymaker), and critical examination of AI bias inheritance and fairness concerns in financial decision-making systems (Ethicist).

This experimental paper requires evaluation of causal inference and identification strategy (Econometrician), assessment of data processing and LLM implementation methodology (Data_Scientist), and critical analysis of financial stability implications and policy relevance (Policymaker).
